title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Query Trace: Data Lifecycle
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql data lifecycle increment in a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Data Lifecycle · Theory Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 4 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Data Lifecycle contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Data Lifecycle · Theory Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Predict Data Lifecycle · Theory Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: A theory support system has 4 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Data Lifecycle condition decided your prediction.

Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy. That claim does not survive the predict data lifecycle evidence boundary.

steps.title: Bound Data Lifecycle · Theory Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 5 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Data Lifecycle application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

content.text: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example support system has 4 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary. A defensible solution applies Data Lifecycle, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record.

content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy. This fails the Data Lifecycle contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Data Lifecycle evidence · Theory Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-data-lifecycle-1bnr5g" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Data Lifecycle evidence · Theory Data Lifecycle.

steps.title: Repair the Data Lifecycle defect · Theory Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory support system has 4 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

A teammate acts on this belief: “Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy.” Which repair addresses the cause?

Evidence target: Repair the Data Lifecycle defect · Theory Data Lifecycle.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
